source.title: Engineering of mCherry variants with long Stokes shift, red-shifted fluorescence, and low cytotoxicity

initial_request.research_problem: Engineering mCherry variants with low cytotoxicity and altered spectral properties (long Stokes shift and red-shifted fluorescence) for live-cell imaging.
initial_request.known_context: mCherry is a widely used red fluorescent protein but exhibits cytotoxicity in E. coli, limiting its utility. mCherry2 is a less cytotoxic variant with similar spectra and slightly higher brightness.
constraints: Long Stokes shift or red-shifted fluorescence
quantifiable_goals: Long Stokes shift variant with excitation ~450 nm and emission ~610 nm
Red-shifted variant with excitation ~600 nm and emission ~630 nm
Brightness comparable to or better than existing RFPs

evolution_trajectory.state_before: mCherry exhibits cytotoxicity in E. coli, limiting its utility as a fluorescent protein.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Need for a less cytotoxic template to engineer new spectral variants.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: mCherry2, a less cytotoxic variant with similar spectra and slightly higher brightness, can serve as a suitable template for directed evolution.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Select mCherry2 as the starting template for engineering long Stokes shift and red-shifted variants.
evolution_trajectory.observation: mCherry2 shows similar growth rates to EGFP in bacterial cytotoxicity assay, indicating lower cytotoxicity than mCherry.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: With a low-cytotoxicity template chosen, hypothesis-driven mutagenesis can be performed to introduce long Stokes shift.

evidence.quote_or_span: bacteria expressing mCherry2 (Fig 2D) showed similar growth rates with bacteria expressing EGFP in the time frame of this test, suggesting they had lower cytotoxicity than mCherry
Due to the high brightness and low cytotoxicity, mCherry2 was chosen as the template for subsequent development of long Stokes shift and red-shifted variants.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: mCherry2 selected as template; no long Stokes shift variant exists.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: How to introduce a long Stokes shift into mCherry2?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Introducing an excited state proton transfer (ESPT) pathway via mutations at residues 161 and 163 will create a long Stokes shift variant.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Perform site-directed saturation mutagenesis at residues 161 and 163 of mCherry2.
evolution_trajectory.observation: The best variant, LSSmCherry0.1 (I161S/Q163E), exhibits blue-shifted excitation but mixed green/red emission.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: The mixed emission indicates failure to achieve pure long Stokes shift; further mutagenesis needed to suppress unwanted fluorescence.

evidence.quote_or_span: site-directed saturation mutagenesis was performed at residues 161 and 163 of mCherry2 (numbered according to DsRed) in an effort to introduce an excited state proton transfer (ESPT) pathway

further spectroscopic characterization of LSSmCherry0.1 revealed that a substantial portion of the protein formed a green fluorescent chromophore, and a substantial amount of short Stokes shift red fluorescence co-existed with the long Stokes shift fluorescence

saturation mutagenesis was performed at position 143, which is in the close vicinity of the chromophore. The variant with the highest ratio of long Stokes shift fluorescence relative to short Stokes shift and green fluorescence was mCherry2-W143L/I161S/Q163E, which was designated as LSSmCherry0.2.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: LSSmCherry0.1 has undesirable green and short Stokes shift red fluorescence.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: How to eliminate the unwanted fluorescence species?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Mutating position 143, near the chromophore, may suppress green and short Stokes shift fluorescence.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Perform saturation mutagenesis at position 143 on LSSmCherry0.1.
evolution_trajectory.observation: The variant mCherry2-W143L/I161S/Q163E (LSSmCherry0.2) shows exclusively long Stokes shift red fluorescence.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: LSSmCherry0.2 is a pure long Stokes shift variant but may need brightness improvement.

evidence.quote_or_span: saturation mutagenesis was performed at position 143, which is in the close vicinity of the chromophore. The variant with the highest ratio of long Stokes shift fluorescence relative to short Stokes shift and green fluorescence was mCherry2-W143L/I161S/Q163E, which was designated as LSSmCherry0.2.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: LSSmCherry0.2 has pure long Stokes shift but unknown brightness.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Can brightness be improved while maintaining long Stokes shift and low cytotoxicity?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Multiple rounds of error-prone PCR (EP-PCR) and screening will yield brighter variants.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Subject LSSmCherry0.2 to multiple rounds of EP-PCR and screen for brightness and low cytotoxicity.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Final variant LSSmCherry1 (mCherry2-A6T/W143L/I161S/Q163E/A217S) obtained with λex=460 nm, λem=610 nm, and brightness calculated as 10.1 from ε=35,000 M⁻¹cm⁻¹ and Φ=0.29.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Characterize cytotoxicity of LSSmCherry1 to assess if low cytotoxicity is retained.

evidence.quote_or_span: These efforts ultimately led to a long Stokes shift (LSS)-mCherry variant ( $\lambda_{ex} = 460$ nm and $\lambda_{em} = 610$ nm) and a red-shifted (RDS)-mCherry variant ( $\lambda_{ex} = 600$ nm and $\lambda_{em} = 630$ nm).
The molar extinction coefficient ( $\varepsilon$ ) and quantum yield ( $\Phi$ ) of LSSmCherry1 was determined to be 35,000 M $^{-1}$ cm $^{-1}$ and 0.29, respectively (Table 1).
The final variant, LSSmCherry1 (equivalent to mCherry2-A6T/W143L/I161S/Q163E/A217S), has a 160 nm Stokes shift, with an excitation maximum at 450 nm and an emission maximum at 610 nm (Fig 1C and 1D).

saturation mutagenesis was performed at position 143, which is in the close vicinity of the chromophore. The variant with the highest ratio of long Stokes shift fluorescence relative to short Stokes shift and green fluorescence was mCherry2-W143L/I161S/Q163E, which was designated as LSSmCherry0.2.
The variant with the highest ratio of long Stokes shift fluorescence relative to short Stokes shift and green fluorescence was mCherry2-W143L/I161S/Q163E, which was designated as LSSmCherry0.

In the attempt to further improve mCherry2-I161S/I163E by elimination of the undesirable green and short Stokes shift red fluorescence, saturation mutagenesis was performed at position 143, which is in the close vicinity of the chromophore.

evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Does LSSmCherry1 retain the low cytotoxicity of mCherry2?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: LSSmCherry1 may have lost low cytotoxicity during directed evolution.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Perform bacterial cytotoxicity assay on LSSmCherry1.
evolution_trajectory.observation: LSSmCherry1 shows cytotoxicity similar to mCherry, indicating loss of mCherry2's low cytotoxicity.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: The loss of low cytotoxicity is a significant drawback; parallel efforts on red-shifted variants may yield better outcomes.

evidence.quote_or_span: The bacterial cytotoxicity assay demonstrated that RDSmCherry1 has cytotoxicity similar to that of mCherry and LSSmCherry1

evolution_trajectory.state_before: During screening of the 161/163 library for long Stokes shift variants, some colonies appeared blue/purple.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Could these unusual colonies represent red-shifted variants?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: The blue/purple colonies may contain red-shifted fluorescent proteins.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Characterize the blue/purple colonies from the 161/163 library.
evolution_trajectory.observation: A variant mCherry2-I161G/Q163G (RDSmCherry0.1) is identified with red-shifted excitation at 598 nm and emission at 625 nm.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: RDSmCherry0.1 is a promising red-shifted variant; further red-shift may be achieved by additional mutations.

evidence.quote_or_span: it was noticed that a number of colonies appeared blue or purple to visual inspection
Gene sequencing revealed this variant to be mCherry2-I161G/Q163G
spectral analysis confirmed that it had a substantially red shifted fluorescence excitation and emission maxima at 598 nm and 625 nm
During screening of the mCherry2 161X/163X library it was noticed that a number of colonies appeared blue or purple to visual inspection.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: RDSmCherry0.1 has red-shifted spectra but further red-shift is desired.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Can π-π stacking interactions further red-shift the fluorescence?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Introducing π-π stacking via mutations at positions 195 and 197 will further red-shift fluorescence.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Perform saturation mutagenesis at positions 195 and 197 on RDSmCherry0.1.
evolution_trajectory.observation: The best variant RDSmCherry0.2 (V195C/I197Y) shows modest red-shift to λex=600 nm, λem=630 nm.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: The red-shift is modest; further rounds of evolution may enhance it.

evidence.quote_or_span: positions 197 and 195 were randomized by saturation mutagenesis
Screening for the most red-shifted mutant in the 195X/197X library led to the identification of mCherry2-I161G/Q163G/V195C/I197Y
this variant was only modestly red-shifted relative to RDSmCherry0.1, with an excitation maximum at 600 nm and an emission maximum at 630 nm

evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Can additional rounds of evolution further red-shift the spectra?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Multiple rounds of saturation mutagenesis and EP-PCR will yield further red-shifted variants.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Subject RDSmCherry0.2 to multiple rounds of saturation mutagenesis and EP-PCR.
evolution_trajectory.observation: RDSmCherry0.5 obtained with λex=604 nm, λem=636 nm, but exhibits undesirable blue fluorescence (λex=400 nm, λem=462 nm).
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: The blue fluorescence is an artifact that needs to be eliminated.

evidence.quote_or_span: Multiple additional rounds of saturation mutagenesis and EP-PCR produced RDSmCherry0.5 with excitation maximum further red-shifted to 604 nm and emission maximum shifted to 636 nm
it was found that this variant exhibited a distinctive blue fluorescence with excitation at 400 nm and emission at 462 nm

evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Mutations at positions 195 and 197 are responsible for the blue fluorescence.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Analyze the mutational origin of blue fluorescence by comparing RDSmCherry0.5 with RDSmCherry0.1.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Blue fluorescence is attributed to mutations at 195 and 197, as RDSmCherry0.1 lacks it.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: To eliminate blue fluorescence, re-mutagenize positions 195/197 on RDSmCherry0.5.

evidence.quote_or_span: This led us to speculate that the mutations at position 195 and 197 were responsible for the undesirable blue fluorescence
5 had 7 mutations: Val16Ser, Ala44Cys, Ala145Pro, Ile161Gly, Gln163Gly, Val195Cys, and Ile197Tyr.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Blue fluorescence in RDSmCherry0.5 linked to positions 195/197.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Can the blue fluorescence be eliminated while retaining far-red fluorescence?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Saturation mutagenesis at 195/197 on RDSmCherry0.5, screening for maximal far-red and minimal blue, will yield a pure red-shifted variant.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Perform saturation mutagenesis at 195 and 197 on RDSmCherry0.5 with dual screening.
evolution_trajectory.observation: RDSmCherry1 (mCherry2-V16S/A44C/A145P/I161G/Q163G/V195C) identified; reversion I197Y eliminates blue fluorescence, yielding λex=600 nm, λem=630 nm.

evidence.quote_or_span: another round of saturation mutagenesis on 195 and 197 was performed on the template of RDSmCherry0.5 with screening for maximal far-red fluorescence and minimal blue fluorescence
Thorough screening of this library led to the identification of mCherry2-V16S/A44C/A145P/I161G/Q163G/V195C, which was designated as RDSmCherry1
Reversion of Tyr197 to Ile in the final round of engineering was associated with the complete disappearance of the blue fluorescence and an increase in red intensity

These efforts ultimately led to a long Stokes shift (LSS)-mCherry variant ( $\lambda_{ex} = 460$ nm and $\lambda_{em} = 610$ nm) and a red-shifted (RDS)-mCherry variant ( $\lambda_{ex} = 600$ nm and $\lambda_{em} = 630$ nm).
Screening for the most red-shifted mutant in the 195X/197X library led to the identification of mCherry2-I161G/Q163G/V195C/I197Y, which was designated RDSmCherry0.

evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: RDSmCherry1 may have lost low cytotoxicity during directed evolution.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Perform bacterial cytotoxicity assay on RDSmCherry1.
evolution_trajectory.observation: RDSmCherry1 has cytotoxicity similar to mCherry, indicating loss of mCherry2's low cytotoxicity.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Both LSSmCherry1 and RDSmCherry1 fail to maintain low cytotoxicity; overall utility is limited.

evidence.quote_or_span: The bacterial cytotoxicity assay demonstrated that RDSmCherry1 has cytotoxicity similar to that of mCherry and LSSmCherry1
indicating that the decreased cytotoxicity of mCherry2 was lost during directed evolution

evolution_trajectory.state_before: LSSmCherry1 and RDSmCherry1 have been engineered but with cytotoxicity issues.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: How do these variants compare to existing red fluorescent proteins?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: The new variants may not be competitive with existing RFPs in brightness or cytotoxicity.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Compare LSSmCherry1 and RDSmCherry1 with existing RFPs like mCardinal and TagRFP657.
evolution_trajectory.observation: LSSmCherry1 and RDSmCherry1 are not improved over existing RFPs in brightness or cytotoxicity, limiting immediate utility.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: The variants may serve as templates for further engineering but are not immediately useful.

evidence.quote_or_span: Similarly, red-shifted variants of the sea anemone-derived mKate RFP, including mCardinal ( $\lambda_{ex} = 604$ nm and $\lambda_{em} = 659$ nm) [30] and TagRFP657 ( $\lambda_{ex} = 611$ nm and $\lambda_{em} = 657$ nm) [31], have been developed.

These efforts ultimately led to a long Stokes shift (LSS)-mCherry variant ( $\lambda_{ex} = 460$ nm and $\lambda_{em} = 610$ nm) and a red-shifted (RDS)-mCherry variant ( $\lambda_{ex} = 600$ nm and $\lambda_{em} = 630$ nm).

validation_methods: Spectral characterization (excitation/emission maxima, extinction coefficient, quantum yield)
metrics: Bacterial growth rate relative to EGFP
success_verification.final_conclusion: LSSmCherry1 and RDSmCherry1 were successfully engineered with desired spectral properties but lost the low cytotoxicity of the mCherry2 template, and are not competitive with existing RFPs.
limitations: Both variants exhibit cytotoxicity similar to mCherry, losing the low cytotoxicity advantage of mCherry2.
Brightness of RDSmCherry1 (5.0) is lower than some existing far-red proteins.
No in vivo imaging validation beyond bacterial cytotoxicity.
